title: draba alpina var. algida
intro: Draba alpina var. algida, also known as Alpine Whitlow Grass or Draba algida, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve congestion and promote the removal of mucus from the lungs. Traditionally, Draba alpina var. algida is prepared as a tea or tincture, and has also been used in herbal remedies to help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
